---
title: Quarto for research
subtitle: Beginner tutorial on using Quarto to share and organised research, introducing learners to GitHub, Quarto and Markdown.
image: homepage.png
---

<br>

I am delivering an in-person workshop for the **Department of Health and Community Sciences** at the **University of Exeter** on **1 June 2026 (1–4 pm)**. The session introduces researchers to **Quarto**, **GitHub**, and **Markdown** as tools for sharing and organising research more openly and reproducibly.

All materials are being developed openly and will remain available online:

* Workshop site: <https://pythonhealthdatascience.github.io/quarto-tutorial/>
* Workshop site GitHub: <https://github.com/pythonhealthdatascience/quarto-tutorial/>
* Template/Codespace used in workshop: <https://github.com/pythonhealthdatascience/quarto-template>

The aim is to provide a simple, reusable starting point for building Quarto websites that can serve as research homepages, project sites, or documentation hubs.

<br>

<iframe
  class="auto-preview"
  width="100%"
  height="500"
  src="https://pythonhealthdatascience.github.io/quarto-tutorial/"
  title="Introduction to Quarto for Research Projects">
</iframe>
